specs.content_brief: The abstract argues that the hypothesis of a cause external to physical reality receives positive qualitative confirmation from a purely a priori principle: positing a single external ground leaves fewer unexplained contingent facts than brute existence, and hypotheses that minimize unexplained contingency are intrinsically more credible. The load-bearing premise is this conceptual probability principle about unexplained contingency. The abstract includes one passing mention of modern cosmology framed explicitly as illustration; the deletion test must come out clean — strike the cosmological aside and the argument runs unchanged, so the type is alpha. The conclusion is stated as qualitative evidential support ('better supported', 'more credible'), not mere coherence and not a numeric claim.
specs.topic_domain: cosmological contingency (S1)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches coders who default to beta whenever cosmological vocabulary appears, even though the empirical aside is deletable and the argument is purely conceptual.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
cosmological reference allowed only as an explicitly illustrative aside
no numeric probabilities or odds (qualitative evidential language only)

specs.content_brief: The abstract develops a purely a priori Bayesian case for an external cause of the physical universe: an invented structural-simplicity measure over hypotheses yields prior odds of at least 20 to 1 favoring an externally caused universe over brute contingency before any observation is consulted. Because no evidence is ever consulted, the posterior just is this prior, so the abstract's explicit primary thesis is a first-order on-balance verdict: an externally caused universe is more credible than brute contingency. The simplicity measure is subordinate/instrumental, not the paper's contribution, so the item is S1 (not M). No empirical datum may carry evidential weight; deleting every empirical reference leaves the derivation intact, so the type is alpha. The explicit odds figure must appear verbatim (CS5).
specs.topic_domain: a priori Bayesian cosmology (S1)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches the assumption that explicit Bayesian numbers signal empirical (beta) support — here the odds are derived from an a priori simplicity measure, so the correct type is alpha with strength CS5.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
an explicit prior-odds figure (at least 20:1 or equivalent) must appear in the abstract
no empirical premise may carry any evidential weight

the on-balance credence claim (posterior equals prior, since no evidence is consulted: an external cause is more credible than brute contingency) must be the explicit primary thesis, with the simplicity measure subordinate/instrumental so the item codes S1, not M

specs.content_brief: The abstract argues on conceptual grounds alone that if physical reality has an external cause, that cause is more credibly a personal agent: a non-agential necessary cause would necessitate its effect, making the universe itself necessary, whereas only intention-based causation can yield a contingent effect from a necessary source. The load-bearing premise is the conceptual analysis of necessitation versus agency; the S1 claim (that there is an external cause) is presupposed, not defended, so the coded step is S2. Any mention of human decision-making is a decorative analogy whose deletion leaves the argument untouched (alpha). The conclusion claims positive qualitative support for the personal-agent hypothesis over impersonal alternatives.
specs.topic_domain: agent causation and the first cause (S2)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches conflation of S2 with S1 when first-cause vocabulary is prominent — the thesis actually defended is the personhood of the cause, with the external cause presupposed.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
the existence of an external cause (S1) is presupposed, never argued for
no numeric probabilities or odds

specs.content_brief: Framed as a reply to unnamed recent critics who charge that agential explanations of the cosmos are explanatorily empty, the abstract's primary contribution is constructive: it argues a priori that intentional explanation uniquely combines contingency-preservation with selectivity among possible outcomes, so the personal-cause hypothesis gains positive qualitative evidential support beyond merely surviving the objection. The net effect through the reply chain is pro-S2, and the abstract must make clear that the constructive evidential claim, not the rebuttal, is the paper's main thesis (hence CS4, not CS2). All premises are conceptual; no empirical or historical premise appears, so the deletion test is vacuous and the type is alpha.
specs.topic_domain: intentional explanation of the cosmos (S2, reply chain)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches miscoding of reply-chain items as CS2 defeater-rebuttal when the paper's primary contribution is a positive evidential claim that goes beyond rebutting the objection.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
the critics replied to must remain generic and unnamed
the rebuttal must be explicitly subordinate to the constructive evidential thesis
no numeric probabilities or odds

specs.content_brief: The abstract argues that an invented grounding-discernibility principle positively confirms the uniqueness and underived status of any ultimate ground: if there were two ultimates, their distinctness would itself be a further fact demanding a ground, contradicting their ultimacy, so hypotheses positing exactly one a se ground are qualitatively better confirmed than multi-ultimate rivals. The load-bearing premise is the discernibility-grounding principle, a piece of pure metaphysics. No empirical premise occurs anywhere, so the deletion test is vacuous and the type is alpha. The conclusion must be stated in evidential terms ('strongly favors') rather than as a bare possibility or coherence claim.
specs.topic_domain: aseity and uniqueness of the ultimate (S3)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches coders who default S3 aseity items to CS1 coherence — the abstract's claim is positive evidential confirmation of uniqueness, not mere consistency of the concept.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
no empirical content of any kind
no numeric probabilities or odds

specs.content_brief: The abstract offers a grounding-theoretic argument that dependence orderings are more credibly well-founded than infinitely descending: it defends an invented transmission principle on which an unbounded descent of dependents transmits being to no member, and concludes that any dependence ordering, whatever it contains, is more credibly terminated in an uncreated terminus than not. The existence of dependent things is stipulated as common ground shared by every compared hypothesis, so it is non-evidential background and does not count under the deletion test (codebook §4-D4, v1.2a); the conclusion is structural, not an inference from the datum that dependents exist. The load-bearing premise is the a priori transmission principle within the formal theory of grounding, and the thesis is first-order metaphysics (S3), not a proposal about method (M). No empirical premise carries weight, so the type is alpha.
specs.topic_domain: grounding and well-foundedness (S3)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches miscoding as M (methodology of case-making) triggered by heavy explanatory-structure vocabulary, when the thesis is first-order metaphysics about an uncreated ground.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
thesis must be first-order metaphysics, not a methods or meta-level proposal
no numeric probabilities or odds
the existence of dependent things is stipulated as common ground shared by all compared hypotheses (non-evidential background; not load-bearing under the deletion test per §4-D4 v1.2a)
the conclusion must be structural (any dependence ordering is more credibly well-founded than not), not an inference drawn from the observed datum that dependent things exist

specs.content_brief: The abstract argues a priori that among hypotheses about an ultimate ground's character, perfect goodness is the only non-arbitrary attribute assignment: any bounded degree of goodness would introduce an unexplained parameter, whereas maximal goodness is a natural zero-parameter limit, so the hypothesis that the ultimate is perfectly good receives positive qualitative confirmation. The load-bearing premise is the conceptual claim that arbitrary parameters lower intrinsic credibility. The thesis defended must be specifically the goodness step, with the existence of an ultimate ground presupposed rather than argued, so the coded step is S4 and not B. No empirical premise may appear; the deletion test is vacuous and the type is alpha.
specs.topic_domain: perfect goodness of the ultimate (S4)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches drift from S4 to B when divine attributes are discussed — only the goodness thesis is defended, with the rest of the ultimate's profile presupposed.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
existence of an ultimate ground is presupposed, never argued for
no numeric probabilities or odds

specs.content_brief: The abstract defends the conditional step itself: supposing, without argument, that God raised Jesus, an invented a priori principle of unique-event identity individuation implies that a singular act of restoring one to indestructible life can be performed by no agent other than the ultimate ground itself, so the raised one is individuated as identical with that ground, and the communicative structure of such an act conceptually commits to internal differentiation within the divine life. The load-bearing premises are conceptual theses about event-individuation, identity, and communicative action; divine action is NOT framed as ratification or endorsement of Jesus's self-presentation. The resurrection functions only as a suppositional antecedent and must never be supported by testimony, documents, or historiography. Deletion test: remove all historical material and the conditional argument still runs, so the type is alpha. The conclusion claims positive qualitative support for the inference from resurrection to incarnation and Trinity.
specs.topic_domain: resurrection-to-incarnation-and-Trinity inference (S8)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches the reflex gamma coding of resurrection-themed items when the historical premise is merely suppositional and the load-bearing premises are wholly conceptual.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
the resurrection appears only as an undefended supposition — no appeal to testimony, documents, or historical evidence
no numeric probabilities or odds

the conceptual bridge must be an invented principle (e.g. unique-event individuation of divine identity); do not frame divine action as ratification or endorsement of Jesus's self-presentation (avoids the divine-signature/ratification argument)

specs.content_brief: The abstract mounts a purely a priori cumulative case for full theism as a bundled hypothesis (one personal, uncreated, perfectly good, providentially active being): an invented parameter-counting calculus assigns full theism a prior probability of approximately 0.6 against its nearest structured rivals, and this explicit figure must appear. Because no evidence is consulted, the posterior just is this prior, so the explicit primary thesis is a first-order on-balance credence claim: full theism is more credible than its nearest rivals. The parameter-counting calculus is instrumental and subordinate to that first-order claim, so the item codes B (not M). No empirical or historical datum may contribute; any mention of the world's existence is schematic and deletable, so the type is alpha. The item targets the whole bundle at once, so the step is B.
specs.topic_domain: cumulative a priori natural theology (B)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches coders who decompose a bundled full-God cumulative case into a single step code (e.g. S1 or S3), and coders who route the invented calculus to M — the first-order credence claim about full theism is the explicit primary thesis, so the code is B.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
an explicit prior-probability value must appear in the abstract
no empirical or historical datum may carry evidential weight

the first-order on-balance credence claim about full theism (posterior equals prior, since no evidence is consulted) must be the explicit primary thesis; the parameter-counting calculus is instrumental and subordinate, so the item codes B, not M

specs.content_brief: The abstract argues that careful conceivability of a maximal being provides defeasible positive evidence of its possibility, and that for maximal beings, evidence of possibility raises the credibility of actuality via an invented modal ascent principle — so full theism ends up more credible than not on wholly a priori grounds. The abstract must explicitly claim positive evidential support, going beyond a coherence or mere-possibility verdict, while stopping short of demonstrative proof and using no numbers. The load-bearing premises are the conceivability-to-evidence link and the modal ascent principle, both conceptual; no empirical premise appears, so the deletion test is vacuous and the type is alpha. The target is the bundled maximal-being hypothesis, so the step is B.
specs.topic_domain: modal-epistemic maximal-being argument (B)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches CS1 miscoding driven by possibility-talk — the abstract's explicit claim is positive evidential support for theism, not mere coherence or possibility.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
must explicitly claim positive evidential support, not mere coherence or possibility
no numeric probabilities or odds

specs.content_brief: The abstract argues that the observed architecture of biological suffering — predation, parasitism, and high juvenile mortality built into the physiology of sentient animals over deep evolutionary time — is positive evidence that any ultimate ground of reality is not perfectly good. The load-bearing premise is the observational record of this suffering's scale and apparent gratuitousness; delete that empirical premise and no case against the ultimate's goodness remains, fixing the item as beta. The move must be evidential — the data lower the probability of a perfectly good ultimate — not a claim of outright logical contradiction.
specs.topic_domain: problem of evil — animal/evolutionary suffering (goodness of the ultimate)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches coders who code an evidential animal-suffering argument as CS1/alpha (the logical problem of evil) rather than CS4/beta.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
load-bearing premise must be observational data on biological suffering (deleting it collapses the argument: beta)
frame as evidence lowering the probability of a good ultimate, not a logical contradiction (CS4, not CS1)
no explicit numbers, probabilities, or Bayes factors

specs.content_brief: The abstract reports a longitudinal empirical study tracking cohorts of earnest self-identified seekers who undertake sustained practices aimed at divine encounter, and argues that the observed pattern — a large, stable fraction reporting no encounter across years despite continued openness — is positive evidence against a good ultimate that engages in special post-creation self-disclosure to persons. The load-bearing premise is the longitudinal observational data on seeker outcomes; delete that observational premise and the argument collapses, so the item is beta rather than purely conceptual. Direction is contra the S5 thesis, and because the argument turns on hiddenness routed to special self-disclosure it carries flag H.
specs.topic_domain: divine hiddenness — longitudinal outcomes of earnest seekers (special divine self-disclosure)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches coders who route hiddenness to S1/S2 (existence) or code it alpha, instead of S5/beta with flag H.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
target the S5 self-disclosure thesis and carry flag H (hiddenness edge anchor)
load-bearing premise must be the longitudinal observational data on earnest-seeker outcomes (beta)
no explicit numbers (CS4)
do not use the phrase 'non-resistant nonbelief' or any signature coinage from the published hiddenness literature; the empirical premise must be a distinct, invented seeker-outcome dataset

specs.content_brief: The abstract reports a controlled outcome study of petitionary/intercessory prayer and argues the measured result is positive evidence against efficacious special divine action. It must state an explicit HYPOTHESIS-LEVEL figure — a Bayes factor or posterior odds favoring the no-special-action hypothesis over the efficacious-prayer hypothesis — and that thesis-level number is what carries the argument; a bare data-level statistic (effect size, relative risk, p-value, outcome ratio) does not satisfy CS5. The underlying trial data are load-bearing: delete them and nothing remains, so the item is beta. The explicit hypothesis-level number makes this CS5, and the direction is contra S5.
specs.topic_domain: petitionary/intercessory prayer efficacy (special divine action)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches coders who accept a bare data-level statistic (p-value, effect size, outcome ratio) as CS5; here the load-bearing figure is a hypothesis-level Bayes factor/posterior odds comparing the two hypotheses, which is what makes the item CS5 rather than CS4.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis

must state an explicit hypothesis-level number — a Bayes factor or posterior odds comparing the no-special-action hypothesis with the efficacious-prayer hypothesis (a bare p-value, effect size, relative risk, or outcome ratio does not satisfy CS5)
the underlying trial data must be load-bearing (beta; deleting them leaves no argument)

the trial must be invented and generic — no named institutions, conditions, or endpoints evoking any real multi-site intercessory-prayer study; the thesis-level Bayes-factor overlay is presented as the paper's own novel analysis of the invented trial

specs.content_brief: The abstract argues that the concretely observed character of physical law — blind, exceptionless, statistically uniform regularity with no trace of agent-like responsiveness or purpose-tracking — is positive evidence that the ground of physical order, if there is one, is impersonal rather than a personal agent. The load-bearing premise is the empirical description of how physical regularities actually behave; delete that observational premise and the argument does not run, fixing it as beta. The target is the personhood of the cause (S2), the direction is contra, and the reasoning is evidential rather than a conceptual claim about what a cause must be.
specs.topic_domain: natural theology — character of physical law (personal-agent ground)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches coders who assign S1 (existence of a cause) when the item targets S2 (its personhood), or who code it alpha.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
target the personhood of the cause (S2), not the existence of a cause (S1)
load-bearing premise must be the observed behavior of physical law (beta)
no explicit numbers (CS4)

specs.content_brief: The abstract marshals observational cosmological data — for instance measured spatial flatness and the specific character of the earliest observable spectrum — as fitting an invented self-sufficient origin model on which physical reality's earliest structure is internally determined by a closed causal loop (a self-normalizing initial sector) and requires no external cause, and treats that observational fit as positive evidence against S1. The load-bearing premise is the observational data supporting the self-sufficient model; delete it and only untethered metaphysics remains, so the argument fails and the item is beta. The direction is contra the S1 thesis (an external cause or ground of physical reality), and the claim is evidential rather than a neutral survey of models.
specs.topic_domain: cosmology — origin of physical reality (external cause/ground)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches coders who read a self-originating-cosmology item as neutral model-comparison or as alpha, rather than contra-S1/CS4/beta.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
direction must be contra S1 (against an external cause/ground), not a neutral comparison
load-bearing premise must be observational cosmological data (beta)
no explicit numbers (CS4)

the self-sufficiency mechanism must be invented (a closed causal loop or self-normalizing initial sector); do not use 'boundaryless'/'no-boundary' phrasing or evoke any recognizable real cosmological proposal

specs.content_brief: This is a reply-chain item: a soul-making style theodicy has claimed that observed suffering is justified because it reliably yields compensating moral or spiritual growth. The abstract presents new observational data — categories of suffering that produce no such growth (pre-conscious infant suffering, deaths that foreclose any developmental arc, large-scale ecological waste), which the theodicy predicts should be rare or absent — and argues that their presence both defeats the theodicy and stands as positive evidence against a perfectly good ultimate. The positive-evidence-against-S4 claim is the explicit primary/closing thesis; the defeat of the theodicy is explicitly subordinate to it. Net direction on the S4 thesis is contra; the load-bearing premise is the observed no-growth suffering (delete it and nothing remains, so beta); carry flag CHAIN and make the net anti-S4 effect explicit.
specs.topic_domain: theodicy debate — soul-making reply chain (goodness of the ultimate)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches coders who let the theodicy framing flip the net direction to pro/neutral, missing the contra-S4 net effect of the reply chain.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
must be a reply chain whose net effect on S4 is contra (flag CHAIN)
load-bearing premise must be the observed no-growth suffering cases (beta)
no explicit numbers (CS4)
the positive-evidence-against-S4 claim must be the explicit primary/closing thesis, with the defeat of the soul-making theodicy explicitly subordinate to it (guards against a CS2 reading)

specs.content_brief: The abstract argues that empirical findings about religious experience — systematically incompatible cross-cultural object-claims together with reproducible neural correlates that arise without any transcendent object — lower the probability of full theism itself: on theism with veridical self-disclosure such systematically conflicting and object-independent experiences are unexpected, whereas they are expected on naturalism, so the data are positive evidence against the bundled full-God hypothesis — not merely that the experiences fail to be veridical. The load-bearing premise is this observational and neuroscientific data; delete it and the argument collapses, fixing the item as beta. Because the argument concerns religious experience bearing on the bundled full-God target, the D1 target is B and it carries flag RE; the direction is contra.
specs.topic_domain: religious experience / neuroscience of religion (bundled full-God target)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches coders who route a religious-experience item to a single step (e.g. S5) or code it pro, and coders who read non-veridicality as a mere undercutting defeater (CS2) — the abstract claims the findings lower the probability of full theism itself, so the code is B/contra/CS4/beta with flag RE.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
D1 target must be B (bundled full-God target) reached via religious experience (flag RE)
load-bearing premise must be observational/neuroscientific data on religious experience (beta)
no explicit numbers (CS4)

the conclusion must be that the findings lower the probability of full theism itself (unexpected on theism with veridical self-disclosure, expected on naturalism), not merely that the experiences are non-veridical (guards against a CS2 undercutting reading)

specs.content_brief: The abstract argues, from textual-critical analysis of the earliest documentary strata, that the empty-tomb and appearance narratives are late accretions absent from the earliest surviving layers and bearing redactional marks of legendary growth, and that this documentary pattern is positive historical evidence against a bodily resurrection. The load-bearing premise is the manuscript-dating and redaction evidence — a historical-documentary premise; delete it and the argument fails, so the item is gamma rather than beta. The direction is contra S7, and the move is positive evidence against the event itself, not a mere rebuttal of an apologetic argument.
specs.topic_domain: resurrection historiography — textual criticism (bodily resurrection)
specs.pitfall_guard: Catches coders who code a documentary/redaction resurrection argument as beta, or as CS2 rebuttal, instead of gamma/CS4 positive evidence against S7.

no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
load-bearing premise must be manuscript-dating/redaction evidence (historical-documentary: gamma)
must be positive historical evidence against the resurrection, not a rebuttal of an apologetic argument (CS4, not CS2)
no explicit numbers (CS4)

specs.pitfall_guard: Catches coders who code a numeric historical-base-rate argument as CS4, or as beta, instead of CS5/gamma.

specs.content_brief: The abstract argues that the measured extreme orderliness of the universe's earliest observable state (inferred from background-radiation uniformity data) is best explained by a cause external to physical reality, since every surveyed internal physical mechanism has been observed to fail to produce such states. The load-bearing premise is the observational measurement of the early low-entropy condition: if that measurement premise were deleted, only a generic causal principle would remain, which by itself yields no conclusion, so the argument collapses (beta). The conclusion must be stated in positive evidential wording ('better explains', 'more probable than not'), never as mere possibility or permissibility.

specs.content_brief: The abstract argues that three independent observational programs — relic-radiation spectrum measurements, primordial element-abundance surveys, and deep galaxy counts, all described generically — each disconfirm eternal-past cosmological models, and that this convergent disconfirmation makes the thesis that physical reality has an external cause more probable than not. The load-bearing premise is the trio of observational results: delete it and the eternal-past rivals stand unchallenged, leaving the external-cause thesis with no support at all, so the argument collapses (beta). specs.content_brief: The abstract presents measurements showing that the parameter region permitting stable, information-processing agents and the much smaller region permitting those agents to communicate intersubjectively (shared, low-noise signaling channels) overlap almost exactly, and argues that this measured double coincidence positively supports the hypothesis that the cause of physical reality is a personal agent who intends a community of communicating minds. The load-bearing premise is the quantified overlap of the agency and intersubjective-communication windows: without that measurement there is no coincidence to explain and the argument collapses (beta). The existence of some external cause is treated as background; the sole thesis defended is the agency of that cause (S2).

specs.content_brief: The abstract opens with a critic's objection that agent hypotheses gain no likelihood advantage from cosmological data because the relevant likelihoods cannot be estimated, then argues from two updated observational datasets that the likelihoods can now be estimated and jointly yield an explicit Bayes factor (an order-of-magnitude figure such as roughly 500 to 1 — the generator may vary the value but MUST state one explicit number) favoring a personal-agent cause over the chance hypothesis. The net effect through the reply chain is pro-S2: the objection is answered and a positive quantitative evidential claim is advanced. The load-bearing premise is the dataset-derived likelihood estimates: delete the data premise and neither the Bayes factor nor the reply survives, so the argument collapses (beta).
specs.topic_domain: S2 — Bayesian confirmation from cosmological datasets: agency vs chance
specs.pitfall_guard: Designed to catch reversing D2 to contra because the abstract foregrounds a critic's objection — the net effect through the reply chain is pro.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts
self-contained single primary thesis
must state one explicit Bayes factor or odds numeral
must open from the critic's objection and close with the author's pro verdict

specs.content_brief: The abstract argues that the observed functional integration of physical order — several independent, separately measured regularities that must jointly co-obtain for any goal-directed system to exist — fits an intending agent-cause better than an impersonal principle, and that this convergent qualitative pattern favors agency. The evidential weight comes from the joint, cross-regularity integration, NOT from any single measured parameter being surprisingly narrow or small on the chance hypothesis. The load-bearing premise is the observed multi-regularity integration: delete it and no pattern remains, so the argument collapses (beta). The comparison is stated qualitatively with no numerals; the sole thesis is the agency of the cause (S2), with the existence of an external cause treated as background.
specs.topic_domain: S2 — functional integration across independently measured regularities (qualitative agent-vs-impersonal comparison)
specs.pitfall_guard: Designed to catch upgrading D3 to CS5 on comparative-likelihood language — comparison without explicit numbers is CS4 — while avoiding the single-parameter surprise-versus-chance move of worked example WE7.
constraints: no real author names or real-paper titles
no verbatim phrases from published abstracts

Per codebook §4-D4 (v1.2a) the stipulated reports are non-load-bearing common ground; the type follows the novel differentiating premise, the psychological-science findings, which are empirical (beta) — delete them and the argument can no longer discriminate resurrection from hallucination. specs.content_brief: The abstract argues, from an analysis of the concept of explanation alone, that positing an external cause of physical reality as a whole is explanatorily self-undermining: whatever explains the totality must either fall under a shared structure (making it internal) or 'explain' in no recognizable sense, so the posit adds an unexplained entity for zero explanatory gain, and by a stated a priori parsimony principle the no-external-cause hypothesis is on balance more credible. The load-bearing premise is the conceptual analysis of what explaining consists in, combined with the a priori parsimony principle. No empirical premise appears anywhere, so the deletion test finds nothing empirical or historical to remove and the argument stands whole — alpha. The conclusion must be a positive comparative-credibility verdict against S1, not a mere coherence worry.

The net direction through the reply chain is contra S2 and must be explicit in the closing claim — the paper goes beyond a defeater rebuttal (CS2) to assert that the rival impersonal hypothesis is more credible (CS4).
